folio.label: 154r
folio.l: ¢ The Sonday next , the Marchaunt was a gon
To Seynt Denys , I comen is Daun Ioún
Witú crovne and berd al fressú , and newe I shaue
In al the hous , there nas so litel a knaue
Ne no wigút elles , that he nas ful fayne
That my lord Daun Ioún , was come a gayne
And shortly to the poynt , rigút for to gon
This faire wyf a corded , witú Daun Ioúnö
That for this hondred frankes , he shulde al nygút
Haue hire in hise armes , bolt vp rigút
And this a cord , ¹formed was in dede
In myrthe al nygút , a besy lif they lede
Til it was day , that Daun Ioún went his way
And bad the meyne , fare wel haue good day
For noon of hem , ne no wigút in the toun
Hatú of Daun Ioún , rigút no suspeccioun
And fortú he ridetú home , to his Abbeye
Or where hi~ list , nomore of hi~ I seye
¢ This Marchau~t , whan that ended was the feire
To Seynt Denys , he gan for to repeire
And witú his wyf , he maketú feste and chere
And telletú hire , that chaffare is so dere
That nedes must he make , a cheuysaunce
For he was bounde , in a reconysaunce
To paie twenty thousand sheldes , a noon
For whicú this Marchau~t , is to Parys gon
To borwe of certeyne frendes , that he hadde
A certeyne frankes , & some witú hi~ he ladde
And whan that he was come , in to the Toun
For gret chierte , and gret affecciou~
Vn to Daun Ioún , he first gotú hi~ to pleye
Nat for to axe , or borwe of hi~ moneye
But for to wyte , and sen of his welfare
And for to tellen hi~ , of his chaffare
As frendes doon , whan they ben met I fere
Daun Ioún hi~ maketú feest , and mery chiere
And he hi~ told a geyn , ful specialy
How he had wel I bougút , and gciously
Thanked by god , al hool his marchandise
Saue that he must , in al manere of wyse
Maken a cheuysau~ce , as for his beste
And thanne he shulde , ben in ioye & reste
Daun Ioún answerde , certes I am fayn
